speaker: LAERTES
text: My necessaries are embarked. Farewell.

speaker: LAERTES
text: And, sister, as the winds give benefit

speaker: LAERTES
text: And convey is assistant, do not sleep,

speaker: LAERTES
text: But let me hear from you.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: Do you doubt that?

speaker: LAERTES
text: For Hamlet, and the trifling of his favor,

speaker: LAERTES
text: Hold it a fashion and a toy in blood,

speaker: LAERTES
text: A violet in the youth of primy nature,

speaker: LAERTES
text: Forward, not permanent, sweet, not lasting,

speaker: LAERTES
text: The perfume and suppliance of a minute,

speaker: LAERTES
text: No more.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: No more but so?

speaker: LAERTES
text: Think it no more.

speaker: LAERTES
text: For nature, crescent, does not grow alone

speaker: LAERTES
text: In thews and bulk, but, as this temple waxes,

speaker: LAERTES
text: The inward service of the mind and soul

speaker: LAERTES
text: Grows wide withal. Perhaps he loves you now,

speaker: LAERTES
text: And now no soil nor cautel doth besmirch

speaker: LAERTES
text: The virtue of his will; but you must fear,

speaker: LAERTES
text: His greatness weighed, his will is not his own,

speaker: LAERTES
text: For he himself is subject to his birth.

speaker: LAERTES
text: He may not, as unvalued persons do,

speaker: LAERTES
text: Carve for himself, for on his choice depends

speaker: LAERTES
text: The safety and the health of this whole state.

speaker: LAERTES
text: And therefore must his choice be circumscribed

speaker: LAERTES
text: Unto the voice and yielding of that body

speaker: LAERTES
text: Whereof he is the head. Then, if he says he loves you,

speaker: LAERTES
text: It fits your wisdom so far to believe it

speaker: LAERTES
text: As he in his particular act and place

speaker: LAERTES
text: May give his saying deed, which is no further

speaker: LAERTES
text: Than the main voice of Denmark goes withal.

speaker: LAERTES
text: Then weigh what loss your honor may sustain

speaker: LAERTES
text: If with too credent ear you list his songs

speaker: LAERTES
text: Or lose your heart or your chaste treasure open

speaker: LAERTES
text: To his unmastered importunity.

speaker: LAERTES
text: Fear it, Ophelia; fear it, my dear sister,

speaker: LAERTES
text: And keep you in the rear of your affection,

speaker: LAERTES
text: Out of the shot and danger of desire.

speaker: LAERTES
text: The chariest maid is prodigal enough

speaker: LAERTES
text: If she unmask her beauty to the moon.

speaker: LAERTES
text: Virtue itself ’scapes not calumnious strokes.

speaker: LAERTES
text: The canker galls the infants of the spring

speaker: LAERTES
text: Too oft before their buttons be disclosed,

speaker: LAERTES
text: And, in the morn and liquid dew of youth,

speaker: LAERTES
text: Contagious blastments are most imminent.

speaker: LAERTES
text: Be wary, then; best safety lies in fear.

speaker: LAERTES
text: Youth to itself rebels, though none else near.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: I shall the effect of this good lesson keep

speaker: OPHELIA
text: As watchman to my heart. But, good my brother,

speaker: OPHELIA
text: Do not, as some ungracious pastors do,

speaker: OPHELIA
text: Show me the steep and thorny way to heaven,

speaker: OPHELIA
text: Whiles, like a puffed and reckless libertine,

speaker: OPHELIA
text: Himself the primrose path of dalliance treads

speaker: OPHELIA
text: And recks not his own rede.

speaker: LAERTES
text: O, fear me not.

speaker: LAERTES
text: I stay too long. But here my father comes.

speaker: LAERTES
text: A double blessing is a double grace.

speaker: LAERTES
text: Occasion smiles upon a second leave.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Yet here, Laertes? Aboard, aboard, for shame!

speaker: POLONIUS
text: The wind sits in the shoulder of your sail,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: And you are stayed for. There, my blessing with thee.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: And these few precepts in thy memory

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Look thou character. Give thy thoughts no tongue,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Nor any unproportioned thought his act.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Be thou familiar, but by no means vulgar.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Those friends thou hast, and their adoption tried,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Grapple them unto thy soul with hoops of steel,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: But do not dull thy palm with entertainment

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Of each new-hatched, unfledged courage. Beware

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Of entrance to a quarrel, but, being in,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Bear ’t that th’ opposèd may beware of thee.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Give every man thy ear, but few thy voice.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Take each man’s censure, but reserve thy judgment.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Costly thy habit as thy purse can buy,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: But not expressed in fancy (rich, not gaudy),

speaker: POLONIUS
text: For the apparel oft proclaims the man,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: And they in France of the best rank and station

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Are of a most select and generous chief in that.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Neither a borrower nor a lender be,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: For loan oft loses both itself and friend,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: And borrowing dulls the edge of husbandry.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: This above all: to thine own self be true,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: And it must follow, as the night the day,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Thou canst not then be false to any man.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Farewell. My blessing season this in thee.

speaker: LAERTES
text: Most humbly do I take my leave, my lord.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: The time invests you. Go, your servants tend.

speaker: LAERTES
text: Farewell, Ophelia, and remember well

speaker: LAERTES
text: What I have said to you.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: ’Tis in my memory locked,

speaker: OPHELIA
text: And you yourself shall keep the key of it.

speaker: LAERTES
text: Farewell.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: What is ’t, Ophelia, he hath said to you?

speaker: OPHELIA
text: So please you, something touching the Lord Hamlet.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Marry, well bethought.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: ’Tis told me he hath very oft of late

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Given private time to you, and you yourself

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Have of your audience been most free and bounteous.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: If it be so (as so ’tis put on me,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: And that in way of caution), I must tell you

speaker: POLONIUS
text: You do not understand yourself so clearly

speaker: POLONIUS
text: As it behooves my daughter and your honor.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: What is between you? Give me up the truth.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: He hath, my lord, of late made many tenders

speaker: OPHELIA
text: Of his affection to me.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Affection, puh! You speak like a green girl

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Unsifted in such perilous circumstance.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Do you believe his “tenders,” as you call them?

speaker: OPHELIA
text: I do not know, my lord, what I should think.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Marry, I will teach you. Think yourself a baby

speaker: POLONIUS
text: That you have ta’en these tenders for true pay,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Which are not sterling. Tender yourself more dearly,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Or (not to crack the wind of the poor phrase,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Running it thus) you’ll tender me a fool.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: My lord, he hath importuned me with love

speaker: OPHELIA
text: In honorable fashion—

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Ay, “fashion” you may call it. Go to, go to!

speaker: OPHELIA
text: And hath given countenance to his speech, my lord,

speaker: OPHELIA
text: With almost all the holy vows of heaven.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Ay, springes to catch woodcocks. I do know,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: When the blood burns, how prodigal the soul

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Lends the tongue vows. These blazes, daughter,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Giving more light than heat, extinct in both

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Even in their promise as it is a-making,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: You must not take for fire. From this time

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Be something scanter of your maiden presence.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Set your entreatments at a higher rate

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Than a command to parle. For Lord Hamlet,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Believe so much in him that he is young,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: And with a larger tether may he walk

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Than may be given you. In few, Ophelia,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Do not believe his vows, for they are brokers,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Not of that dye which their investments show,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: But mere implorators of unholy suits,

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Breathing like sanctified and pious bawds

speaker: POLONIUS
text: The better to beguile. This is for all:

speaker: POLONIUS
text: I would not, in plain terms, from this time forth

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Have you so slander any moment leisure

speaker: POLONIUS
text: As to give words or talk with the Lord Hamlet.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: Look to ’t, I charge you. Come your ways.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: I shall obey, my lord.
